2604.01178.title: Screening Is Enough
2604.01178.paper_summary: A core limitation of standard softmax attention is that it does not define a notion of absolute query--key relevance: attention weights are obtained by redistributing a fixed unit mass across all keys according to their relative scores. As a result, relevance is defined only relative to competing keys, and irrelevant keys cannot be explicitly rejected. We introduce Multiscreen, a language-model architecture built around a mechanism we call screening, which enables absolute query--key relevance. Instead of redistributing attention across all keys, screening evaluates each key against an explicit threshold, discarding irrelevant keys and aggregating the remaining keys, thereby removing global competition among keys. Across experiments, Multiscreen achieves comparable validation loss with approximately 40% fewer parameters than a Transformer baseline, enables stable optimization at substantially larger learning rates, maintains strong performance in long-context perplexity, shows little to no degradation in retrieval performance even far beyond the training context length, and reduces inference latency by up to 3.2$\times$ at 100K context length.
2604.01178.paper_summary_zh: 標準 softmax 注意力的一個核心限制是它並未定義絕對的查詢--鍵相關性：注意力權重是通過根據相對分數在所有鍵之間重新分配固定的單位質量來獲得的。<lb/>因此，相關性僅相對於競爭鍵來定義，而無關鍵無法被明確拒絕。我們引入了 Multiscreen，一種圍繞我們稱之為篩選的機制構建的語言模型架構，該機制使得絕對查詢--鍵相關性成為可能。<lb/>篩選不再在所有鍵之間重新分配注意力，而是將每個鍵與明確的閾值進行評估，丟棄無關鍵並聚合剩餘的鍵，從而消除鍵之間的全局競爭。<lb/>在各項實驗中，Multiscreen 的驗證損失與約 40% 更少的參數相比，達到了可比的結果，並在顯著更大的學習率下實現了穩定的優化，保持了在長上下文困惑度中的強大表現，即使在遠超過訓練上下文長度的情況下，檢索性能幾乎沒有下降，並且在 100K 上下文長度下將推理延遲減少了最多 3.2$\times$。
2604.01178.author: Ken M. Nakanishi et.al.
